source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the physical architecture of a 1500x900x800 mm, <=80 kg autonomous four-wheeled platform with a 10 kg 48 V battery pack and a LiDAR + camera sensor cage at 800 mm height: extruded aluminum space frame (longitudinal rails + crossmembers + A-pillar posts) + welded battery tray + sensor cage tower + firewall bulkhead + suspension mounts + skin panels. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the chassis and battery enclosure of an autonomous self-built mobility vehicle for a university creative-mobility benchmark. The vehicle is a small autonomous four-wheeled platform with external dimensions up to 1500 mm x 900 mm x 800 mm (L x W x H) and dry mass up to 80 kg. The powertrain is battery-electric with a 48 V nominal lithium-ion pack; the battery enclosure must be mechanically rigid (no deflection > 2 mm under a 5 g lateral shock with the pack at full mass) and must include a crush-resistant tray sized for a 24-cell lithium-ion pack with approximately 10 kg pack mass. A mechanical kill-switch must be reachable from outside the vehicle and from the driver's position (if the vehicle has a driver position for fallback control). The chassis must survive: (a) 2.5 g vertical gust under 80 kg gross vehicle mass (196 kg equivalent), (b) 1.5 g lateral cornering, (c) 8 g frontal impact equivalent (640 kg equivalent) — no intrusion of the battery enclosure or the sensor cage. Sensor cage must hold LiDAR + camera rig at 800 mm height and under 2 g vertical shock with sensor displacement < 2 mm (sensor pointing stability). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the four load cases plus a battery-enclosure short-circuit fire test (non-structural, regulatory). Tolerances on suspension mounts are per the rulebook (datasheet per PDF). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at the structural design gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the chassis + battery enclosure + sensor cage, a mass budget, and a report listing dry mass in kg, max chassis stress per case in MPa, battery tray deflection in mm, and sensor cage displacement at 2 g in mm.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The vehicle is delivered as a multi-part assembly: two longitudinal aluminum rails + four crossmembers forming the primary ladder frame, a forward firewall bulkhead that also doubles as the front crumple bulkhead protecting the battery, a welded / bolted battery tray sub-assembly sitting between the two inboard crossmembers, four suspension pickup brackets (one per corner) for the A-arm / wishbone mounts, a sensor cage tower extending to 800 mm with a top sensor platform carrying the LiDAR + camera rig, and outer sheet-metal skin panels (floor pan, side panels, nose cover). The load paths are: LC1/LC2 gross vehicle loads transferred through wheels -> suspension pickup brackets -> longitudinal rails -> crossmembers -> battery tray reaction; LC3 frontal impact propagates from nose skin -> front crossmember -> firewall bulkhead with the hard requirement that the battery tray back wall sees zero intrusion; LC4 battery lateral load reacts directly into the two inboard crossmembers that clamp the tray; LC5 sensor cage acceleration runs down the tower into its mounting flange on the upper rails. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (TIG weld allowables for Al 6061-T6 + bolted joint per RSIB). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a student single-seat electric mobility vehicle chassis as a multi-part assembly for the Korean student mobility competition.

Envelope 1500 x 900 x 800 mm, driver + vehicle mass <= 80 kg, 48 V drivetrain with 10 kg battery pack. Structure Al 6061-T6.

Load cases: LC1 2.5 g vertical bump; LC2 1.5 g cornering; LC3 8 g frontal impact; LC4 5 g lateral on battery pack; LC5 2 g on sensor mounts.

Pass/fail: no yield under any LC with SF 1.5; FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0 at suspension mounts, battery clamps, and seat-belt anchors.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Short-circuit/fire rating is a regulatory battery-pack cert, not structural FEA.

prompt.title: University autonomous-mobility vehicle chassis (multi-part ladder frame + battery tray + sensor tower)
prompt.brief: Design a 1.5 x 0.9 x 0.8 m, <=80 kg autonomous 48 V vehicle as an assembled 6061-T6 space frame: 2 longitudinal rails, 4 crossmembers, firewall bulkhead, welded battery tray for a 10 kg pack, 4 suspension pickup brackets, 800 mm sensor cage tower, skin panels. No battery-box or sensor-cage intrusion under 8 g frontal. FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0 on every TIG weld and bolted interface.

geometric_constraints.ladder_frame_topology: 2 longitudinal rails + 4 crossmembers (front-bumper, front-firewall, rear-inboard, rear-bumper)

material.weld_derating: HAZ yield 0.6 x base for TIG-welded 6061-T6 per Aluminum Design Manual
material.battery_tray_liner: FR-rated fiberglass or mica sheet (electrical + thermal barrier)

load_cases.name: vertical_2p5g
load_cases.description: 80 kg x 2.5 g vertical at all 4 suspension pickups

load_cases.name: lateral_1p5g
load_cases.description: 80 kg x 1.5 g lateral cornering at outboard wheel pickups

load_cases.name: frontal_8g
load_cases.description: 80 kg x 8 g forward applied at the nose crossmember; governs firewall + battery tray intrusion check

load_cases.name: battery_5g_lateral
load_cases.description: 10 kg pack x 5 g lateral applied at the pack CG inside the tray

load_cases.name: sensor_cage_2g_vertical
load_cases.description: 5 kg sensor mass x 2 g vertical applied at the 800 mm top platform

boundary_conditions.wheels: fixed vertical + longitudinal reactions at each of the 4 suspension pickup brackets; lateral restraint at 2 contact patches per cornering case
boundary_conditions.sensor_cage_base: bolted to upper longitudinal rails via 4x M8
boundary_conditions.battery_tray: bolted to crossmembers 2 and 3 via 6x M8 with FR liner between tray and rail

objective.design_heuristic: Keep primary extrusions to 40x40x2 mm or 50x25x2 mm Al 6061-T6 to stay below 80 kg with battery tray and sensor cage included.

parts.name: longitudinal_rail
parts.role: primary load-carrying chassis member running the full 1500 mm length; forms the two sides of the ladder frame and anchors suspension brackets, battery tray, sensor cage, and skin panels

parts.name: crossmember
parts.role: transverse member connecting the two longitudinal rails; the 4 crossmembers are (B1) front bumper crossmember, (B2) firewall/front-battery crossmember, (B3) rear-battery/rear-inboard crossmember, (B4) rear bumper crossmember

parts.name: firewall_bulkhead
parts.role: vertical shear panel welded/bolted between crossmember B2 and the floor pan; isolates battery tray from the forward crumple zone and carries LC3 frontal impact load

geometric_constraints.mounting: bolted to crossmember B2 top flange + bolted to longitudinal rails

parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 sheet or A36 steel sheet with FR liner on battery side

parts.name: battery_tray_subassembly
parts.role: welded aluminum box housing the 24-cell 48 V 10 kg lithium-ion pack between crossmembers B2 and B3; crush-resistant tray with FR liner; clamps pack vertically and laterally
parts.count_note: Treated as a single sub-assembly (floor + 4 walls + lid) for the interface inventory.
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 welded box + FR liner; stainless steel bolts to rails

parts.name: suspension_pickup_bracket
parts.role: corner bracket welded/bolted to a longitudinal rail that receives the A-arm or wishbone suspension mount; reacts vertical and lateral wheel loads into the ladder frame
parts.count_note: One bracket per wheel corner (front-left, front-right, rear-left, rear-right).

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 3x M8 to a-arm inner pivot pair

parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 plate or A36 steel plate (teams choose; steel preferred for fatigue)

parts.name: sensor_cage_tower
parts.role: slender vertical cage rising from the upper rails to 800 mm carrying a top platform that mounts the LiDAR + camera rig; pointing stability governs R5
parts.count_note: Sub-assembly: 4 vertical legs + 2 horizontal braces + top sensor plate.

parts.name: skin_panel
parts.role: non-structural outer covering panels (floor pan, 2 side panels, nose cover, rear cover) mounted to the ladder frame and crossmembers; carries only minor aerodynamic and debris loads

geometric_constraints.mounting: riveted or bolted on 100 mm pitch

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: SF 1.5 yield (base metal); HAZ allowable = 0.67 x 0.6 x 276 MPa at TIG weld zones
No battery-box intrusion under 8 g frontal (hard requirement; firewall C protects battery tray D)

TIG weld DCR per Aluminum Design Manual for HAZ-derated 6061-T6 and bolted-joint DCR per VDI 2230 on every rail-to-crossmember, crossmember-to-bracket, tray-to-rail, and sensor-cage-to-rail interface.
Battery tray must not translate more than 5 mm inboard under LC3 frontal 8 g even after the firewall absorbs primary load; ensures no pack terminal contacts the firewall.
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (rails + crossmembers) + shell (firewall, tray walls, sensor cage, skin) + solid (suspension pickup brackets)
recommended_fea_setup.contact_modeling: tied for TIG weld beads; bolted preload with contact pair at every bolted flange

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG fillet weld around tube intersection (HAZ-derated 6061-T6)
interfaces.load_path: Vertical and lateral wheel loads from suspension into rail, then shear transfer across weld into crossmember

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted flange (4x M8) between firewall bulkhead and front-firewall crossmember B2
interfaces.load_path: LC3 frontal impact load from front bumper crossmember B1 -> rails -> B2 -> firewall bulkhead shear panel

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted joint (3x M8) plus TIG tack welds at suspension pickup bracket to rail
interfaces.load_path: Wheel reaction loads (LC1 vertical, LC2 lateral) from A-arm pivot into bracket and into longitudinal rail

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted connection (6x M8 per tray wall) between battery tray subassembly and crossmembers B2, B3
interfaces.load_path: LC4 battery 5 g lateral reacts into the two inboard crossmembers; LC3 firewall reaction also enters tray via B2

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted base flange (4x M8) of sensor cage tower to upper face of both longitudinal rails
interfaces.load_path: LC5 sensor mass x 2 g vertical + moment arm from 800 mm height into rail pair

interfaces.mediated_by: pop rivets or M4 bolts on 100 mm pitch clamping skin panel to rails/crossmembers
interfaces.load_path: Non-primary aero/debris load; ensures panels do not detach under LC2 lateral

fem_check_policy.rationale: The no-intrusion requirement for the battery tray under 8 g frontal impact is an assembly-scope check that crosses the nose skin, front crossmember, firewall bulkhead, and tray walls; it cannot be verified by isolating any one part. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A 1.5 m autonomous mobility platform with a separable battery pack, swappable sensor rig, and welded aluminum ladder frame is inherently an assembly: the battery tray must be removable for pack service, the sensor tower must be removable for transport, and the suspension pickup brackets are local welded components that are machined separately.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact extrusion section, crossmember positions, and bracket geometry are speculative; the topology (ladder frame + firewall + tray sub-assembly + sensor tower + pickups + skin) is the standard student-mobility architecture.

eval_coverage: bolted battery tray and sensor tower subassemblies

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
